title: The inverse two-point DFT recovers both input coordinates

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The inverse two-point DFT formulas recover both original signal coordinates from the DC and Nyquist modes.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real numbers
DC and Nyquist modes are computed by the two-point transform
informal.expected_conclusion: Both inverse coordinates recover the original input coordinates.

declarations.name: dft2DC
declarations.statement: def dft2DC (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 + u1

declarations.name: dft2Nyquist
declarations.statement: def dft2Nyquist (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 - u1

declarations.name: idft2First
declarations.statement: def idft2First (low high : Real) : Real := (low + high) / 2

declarations.name: idft2Second
declarations.statement: def idft2Second (low high : Real) : Real := (low - high) / 2

declarations.name: dft2_inverse
declarations.statement: theorem dft2_inverse (u0 u1 : Real) : idft2First (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u0 /\ idft2Second (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u1

tasks.input: Translate the two-coordinate inverse property of the two-point DFT into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem dft2_inverse (u0 u1 : Real) : idft2First (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u0 /\ idft2Second (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u1

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof using the two one-coordinate inverse lemmas.
tasks.target: Use And.intro with idft2First_dft2 and idft2Second_dft2.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemmas needed to prove the two-coordinate inverse two-point DFT theorem.
